global.title: Swarm feeds use a versioned ph:v2:<kind>:<id> topic namespace spanning doc, drive, user, profile, and share feeds
global.content:
# Feed Topic Naming Convention (ph:v2:*)

The adapter uses a versioned colon-delimited topic namespace for all Swarm feeds. The `ph:v2:` prefix anchors the schema version and enables forward-compatible migration.

| Feed | Topic pattern | Content |
|------|--------------|---------|
| Document manifest | `ph:v2:doc:<documentId>` | Pointer to encrypted operation batches |
| Drive manifest | `ph:v2:drive:<driveId>` | Documents + folder structure for one drive |
| User manifest | `ph:v2:user:<eth_address>` | Index of all user's drives |
| Public profile | `ph:v2:profile:<address>` | Bee node public key for sharing (unencrypted) |
| Share manifest | `ph:v2:share:<sender>:<recipient>` | Shared drive bundles between users |

Topology is hierarchical: **user manifest → drive manifests → document manifests → encrypted op batches**. On recovery, a client reads the user feed first, then descends to discover all drives and documents.

Profile feeds are the only unencrypted feeds — they publish a Bee node's public key so senders can ACT-encrypt shares to that recipient. Share feeds carry full drive bundles (including folder metadata) from sender to recipient.
global.description: All adapter feeds follow ph:v2:<kind>:<id>: doc, drive, user manifests are encrypted; profile feeds publish an unencrypted public key; share feeds carry encrypted drive bundles between users.
